item.title: Using ChatGPT as a personal assistant
item.description:
In today’s fast-paced world, where time is of the essence, we’re all looking for ways to simplify our lives and get things done more efficiently. That’s where artificial intelligence (AI) comes in — and specifically, ChatGPT.

ChatGPT has taken out the world by storm and I have been using it as my personal assistant for quite a long time.

ChatGPT can be used for a wide range of tasks, from answering simple questions to assisting with complex projects. But how can you make the most of this powerful tool and use it as your very own personal assistant?

In this article, I’ll explore the many ways in which ChatGPT can help you streamline your workflow, save time, and achieve your goals more effectively. 

Whether you’re a busy professional, a student, or simply someone who wants to get more done in less time, you won’t want to miss these valuable insights and tips. So, let’s dive in and discover the power of ChatGPT as a personal assistant!

These are covered in detail in my course as well, with the following course content.

{{ $image := resources.Get “Screenshot_20230417_212536.png” }}

What is a prompt?

A prompt is a set of instructions or guidelines given to a person or a computer program to perform a specific task or to generate a particular response. In the context of natural language processing (NLP), a prompt is a text or voice command that is given to an AI language model to generate a response that is relevant to the prompt. Prompts can be used to train language models, generate text or speech, answer questions, and perform a variety of other tasks in NLP. The quality and specificity of the prompt can have a significant impact on the quality and relevance of the generated response.

How to write effective prompts?

To write better prompts, you should follow these templates

  • Task/Topic: Clearly state the topic or task at hand
  • Intended Audience: Specify the intended audience for the prompt
  • Context/Background Information: Provide necessary context or background information to aid ChatGPT in understanding the prompt
  • Desired Outcome/Goal: Clearly and specifically communicate the desired outcome or goal of the prompt
  • Prompt Modifiers: Use specific prompt modifiers to further clarify the type of response or format desired
  • Examples/Scenarios: Include relevant examples or scenarios, if applicable, to illustrate the desired outcome
  • Criteria/Evaluation Metrics: Include any relevant criteria or evaluation metrics for assessing the response, if applicable
  • Ambiguity Check: Double-check for potential ambiguity or vagueness in the prompt and clarify as necessary

Sample prompts

For College and School Students

  1. Act as my fellow student and help me prepare for my exam. I will be providing a topic, ask me some questions and discussing some relevant points.
  2. Act as an exam question paper that would ask me 1 question at a time for the topic of the Theory of relativity. When I answer, please evaluate it and also provide any improvements.
  3. Act as a deck of flashcards and help me memorize the key concepts related to the theory of relativity
  4. Write a plan for study for me to prepare for the exam for Theory of Relativity that I need to clear in 2 weeks.
  5. Act as a job interview coach. Help me prepare for common interview questions and how to answer them effectively.
  6. Act as a language tutor. Can you explain the difference between prepositions and conjunctions in a simple way?
  7. How do I solve the equation – y=mx+b
  8. Explain Euler’s method.
  9. What is the difference between an acute angle and an obtuse angle?
  10. Can you give me some practice problems on geometry?

For Professionals

  1. Write a LinkedIn post Write tweets from a paragraph and make a thread.
  2. Generate a Reddit post
  3. Write an email marketing campaign to promote your latest ebook.
  4. How can I optimize my website for search engine ranking?
  5. Generate social media content ideas for a new clothing line.
  6. Create a social media advertising campaign for a vegan restaurant.
  7. Write a press release for the launch of a new line of skincare products.
  8. How can I leverage Instagram to increase sales for my online store?
  9. Produce 30 creative slogans for a new line of athletic wear.
  10. Write a blog post about the benefits of using natural beauty products.
  11. Create a Facebook advertising campaign for a travel company.
  12. How can I use Pinterest to drive traffic to my website?
  13. Write a product description for a new line of eco-friendly cleaning products.
  14. Assist me in creating a project timeline for my upcoming project.
  15. Provide a detailed breakdown of tasks, deadlines, and dependencies for each phase of the project, taking into account potential delays and contingencies. Include a Gantt chart or similar visual aid to help me track progress and identify potential bottlenecks.
  16. Research and provide me with a list of the best marketing tools for small businesses.
  17. Conduct thorough research and provide a detailed comparison of the top marketing tools, including their features, pricing, and customer reviews. Offer recommendations based on my business needs and budget, and suggest ways to integrate the tools into my marketing strategy.
  18. Help me write a professional email to my boss asking for a raise.
  19. Provide a template and guidance on how to structure the email, including an attention-grabbing subject line and a clear and concise message outlining my achievements and contributions to the company. Offer tips on how to make a compelling case for a raise while remaining professional and respectful.
  20. Conduct research and provide a comprehensive list of the most effective sales strategies, including cross-selling, upselling, bundling, and referral programs. Offer advice on how to implement these strategies in my business and measure their effectiveness.
  21. Help me identify the strengths and weaknesses of my team members, and develop a tailored plan to improve their skills and knowledge. Provide guidance on how to set SMART goals, create training programs, and track progress.
  22. Write a cover letter for a graphic designer position, emphasizing your creative skills and portfolio.
  23. Generate a personalized objective statement for a human resources resume.
  24. Come up with a list of 5 relevant achievements to include in a marketing manager cover letter.
  25. Generate a tailored 2-minute pitch for an IT project manager job interview.
  26. Write a persuasive email to a potential employer explaining your experience as a sales representative.
  27. Come up with a list of 10 unique qualities to include in an event planner resume.
  28. Generate a 1-page summary of your experiences and accomplishments as a digital marketer.
  29. Write a cover letter addressing the specific qualifications listed for a software development manager position.
  30. Come up with a list of 5 ways to tailor your resume for an accounting job.
  31. Write a compelling property listing for a beachfront villa in Malibu, California.
  32. Generate a list of 10 prospective tenant follow-up messages for a property management company.
  33. Write a persuasive email to a potential buyer for a luxury penthouse in New York City.
  34. Create a 2-minute virtual tour script for a modern condo in downtown Toronto.
  35. Create a list of 5 local schools to mention in a family-friendly neighbourhood guide.
  36. Write a 1-page property brochure for a historic colonial-style home in Boston.
  37. Write a captivating property description for a rustic mountain cabin in Aspen, Colorado.
  38. Come up with a series of 5 Instagram posts to showcase your luxury listings.
  39. Generate a list of 10 home-selling tips for homeowners.
  40. Write a persuasive letter to a property owner about renovating their property before listing with you.
  41. Come up with a list of 5 home remodelling tips for sellers.
  42. Generate a list of 10 potential clients from your social media networks.
  43. Write a persuasive message to a client who is downsizing to a smaller home.
  44. Come up with a series of 5 creative home staging ideas for a small space.
  45. Explain the importance of event planning software and tools, including project management, guest management, and registration.
  46. Write a guide on event decor, including trends, budgeting, and vendor selection.
  47. Explain the different types of event production companies, including audiovisual, lighting, and staging services.
  48. Provide a list of event planning associations and organizations, including benefits and membership requirements.
  49. Offer an in-depth analysis of current event marketing trends, including social media, influencer marketing, and experiential marketing.
  50. Write a guide on event insurance, including types of coverage and how to select the right policy for your event.
  51. Explain the importance of event staffing, including hiring, training, and managing a team for successful event execution.
  52. As a software engineer, design a user interface for a new mobile app, including wireframes, prototypes, and usability testing.
  53. As a financial analyst, conduct a financial analysis for a new investment opportunity, including financial statements, cash flow analysis, and risk assessment.

Personal Assistance

  1. What are some cost-effective ways to improve the curb appeal of my home?
  2. Can you recommend some energy-efficient upgrades to my home to save on utility bills?
  3. What are some DIY projects that I can do to update my bathroom without breaking the bank?
  4. Can you suggest some low-maintenance landscaping ideas for my backyard?
  5. How can I create more storage space in my home without adding square footage?
  6. What are some new movies that are coming out in theatres this month?
  7. Can you recommend a good book for me to read during my upcoming vacation?
  8. I want to plan a weekend getaway with my friends. What are some fun activities we can do in the area?
  9. What are some popular video games that are released this year?
  10. List some funny ways to prank your siblings.
  11. Write a humorous message to your best friend on their wedding day.
  12. Come up with a witty one-liner about pandas.
  13. Write a parody of a popular song about pizza.
  14. What happens when a unicorn and a mermaid go on a date?
  15. Write a short story where a stapler is a protagonist.
  16. Imagine a world where cats can speak. What do they say?
  17. What if aliens landed on Earth and were obsessed with cheeseburgers?
  18. Write a funny dialogue between a pen and a pencil.
  19. Can you recommend a good TV show to binge-watch on Netflix?
  20. What are some effective methods to help my toddler sleep through the night?
  21. My child is having difficulty adjusting to a new school. Can you suggest some strategies to help them cope?
  22. What are some age-appropriate chores that I can assign to my children to teach them responsibility?
  23. Can you recommend some healthy snacks for my child’s lunchbox?
  24. How can I encourage my child to read more and develop a love for books?
  25. I’m planning a trip to Japan and want to experience the cherry blossom season. When is the best time to go?
  26. Can you recommend a guided tour of the ancient ruins in Machu Picchu, Peru?
  27. I want to plan a ski trip to the Swiss Alps for a group of friends in December. Can you suggest some ski resorts and accommodations?
  28. Act as a personal trainer. Help me devise a workout plan for building core strength and stability.
  29. Provide a guide on healthy eating habits for individuals with specific dietary restrictions, such as gluten-free or dairy-free.
  30. Create a 1-month workout plan for improving flexibility and mobility.
  31. Write a comprehensive guide on managing anxiety and stress through mindfulness and meditation techniques.
  32. Provide a list of common mental health disorders and their symptoms, as well as resources for seeking professional help.
  33. Explain the benefits of regular physical activity for cardiovascular health and provide a sample cardio workout plan.
  34. Offer a detailed explanation of the benefits and risks of different types of diets, such as keto and paleo.
  35. Write an in-depth analysis of the current state of mental health care and its impact on marginalized communities.
  36. Provide a list of recommended resources for managing chronic pain and improving quality of life.
  37. Explain the importance of good posture and offer exercises for improving posture and preventing back pain.
  38. Generate some vegan recipe ideas
  39. Create a complete recipe for Vegan Pizza for a single serving
  40. Create a weekly shopping list for the above-mentioned recipe for a single person
  41. Generate a list of vacation ideas that I can go for 5 days from my home city of Melbourne
  42. Generate the complete itinerary for Phillip Island
  43. Create a packing list for this itinerary
  44. Create a 2-week itinerary for a vacation to India
  45. Give me the top 10 stocks in us stock market which has high Dividend yield from 2011 to 2021
  46. I have 100% exposure in equities, what other ways I can look into diversifying my investments
  47. What are some cost-effective ways to improve the facade appeal of my home?
  48. Provide some DIY to improve Landscaping in a total budget of A$1000 with some options from Bunnings
  49. Can you recommend some energy-efficient upgrades to my home to save on utility bills?
  50. I want to plan a weekend getaway with my friends. What are some fun activities we can do in the area?

Conclusion

In conclusion, prompts play a vital role in natural language processing by guiding AI language models to generate specific responses relevant to the given task or topic. The use of prompts has revolutionized the field of NLP, enabling machines to understand and respond to human language more accurately and efficiently. It is important to provide clear and specific prompts to ensure the desired outcome is achieved, and to be aware of potential ambiguities or vagueness in the prompt to avoid generating irrelevant or misleading responses. As NLP continues to evolve, the use of prompts will undoubtedly become more prevalent, allowing us to interact with machines in increasingly natural and intuitive ways.

item.title: Using ChatGPT as a personal assistant
item.description:
In today’s fast-paced world, where time is of the essence, we’re all looking for ways to simplify our lives and get things done more efficiently. That’s where artificial intelligence (AI) comes in — and specifically, ChatGPT.

ChatGPT has taken out the world by storm and I have been using it as my personal assistant for quite a long time.

ChatGPT can be used for a wide range of tasks, from answering simple questions to assisting with complex projects. But how can you make the most of this powerful tool and use it as your very own personal assistant?

In this article, I’ll explore the many ways in which ChatGPT can help you streamline your workflow, save time, and achieve your goals more effectively. 

Whether you’re a busy professional, a student, or simply someone who wants to get more done in less time, you won’t want to miss these valuable insights and tips. So, let’s dive in and discover the power of ChatGPT as a personal assistant!

These are covered in detail in my course as well, with the following course content.

What is a prompt?

A prompt is a set of instructions or guidelines given to a person or a computer program to perform a specific task or to generate a particular response. In the context of natural language processing (NLP), a prompt is a text or voice command that is given to an AI language model to generate a response that is relevant to the prompt. Prompts can be used to train language models, generate text or speech, answer questions, and perform a variety of other tasks in NLP. The quality and specificity of the prompt can have a significant impact on the quality and relevance of the generated response.

How to write effective prompts?

To write better prompts, you should follow these templates

  • Task/Topic: Clearly state the topic or task at hand
  • Intended Audience: Specify the intended audience for the prompt
  • Context/Background Information: Provide necessary context or background information to aid ChatGPT in understanding the prompt
  • Desired Outcome/Goal: Clearly and specifically communicate the desired outcome or goal of the prompt
  • Prompt Modifiers: Use specific prompt modifiers to further clarify the type of response or format desired
  • Examples/Scenarios: Include relevant examples or scenarios, if applicable, to illustrate the desired outcome
  • Criteria/Evaluation Metrics: Include any relevant criteria or evaluation metrics for assessing the response, if applicable
  • Ambiguity Check: Double-check for potential ambiguity or vagueness in the prompt and clarify as necessary

Sample prompts

For College and School Students

  1. Act as my fellow student and help me prepare for my exam. I will be providing a topic, ask me some questions and discussing some relevant points.
  2. Act as an exam question paper that would ask me 1 question at a time for the topic of the Theory of relativity. When I answer, please evaluate it and also provide any improvements.
  3. Act as a deck of flashcards and help me memorize the key concepts related to the theory of relativity
  4. Write a plan for study for me to prepare for the exam for Theory of Relativity that I need to clear in 2 weeks.
  5. Act as a job interview coach. Help me prepare for common interview questions and how to answer them effectively.
  6. Act as a language tutor. Can you explain the difference between prepositions and conjunctions in a simple way?
  7. How do I solve the equation – y=mx+b
  8. Explain Euler’s method.
  9. What is the difference between an acute angle and an obtuse angle?
  10. Can you give me some practice problems on geometry?

For Professionals

  1. Write a LinkedIn post Write tweets from a paragraph and make a thread.
  2. Generate a Reddit post
  3. Write an email marketing campaign to promote your latest ebook.
  4. How can I optimize my website for search engine ranking?
  5. Generate social media content ideas for a new clothing line.
  6. Create a social media advertising campaign for a vegan restaurant.
  7. Write a press release for the launch of a new line of skincare products.
  8. How can I leverage Instagram to increase sales for my online store?
  9. Produce 30 creative slogans for a new line of athletic wear.
  10. Write a blog post about the benefits of using natural beauty products.
  11. Create a Facebook advertising campaign for a travel company.
  12. How can I use Pinterest to drive traffic to my website?
  13. Write a product description for a new line of eco-friendly cleaning products.
  14. Assist me in creating a project timeline for my upcoming project.
  15. Provide a detailed breakdown of tasks, deadlines, and dependencies for each phase of the project, taking into account potential delays and contingencies. Include a Gantt chart or similar visual aid to help me track progress and identify potential bottlenecks.
  16. Research and provide me with a list of the best marketing tools for small businesses.
  17. Conduct thorough research and provide a detailed comparison of the top marketing tools, including their features, pricing, and customer reviews. Offer recommendations based on my business needs and budget, and suggest ways to integrate the tools into my marketing strategy.
  18. Help me write a professional email to my boss asking for a raise.
  19. Provide a template and guidance on how to structure the email, including an attention-grabbing subject line and a clear and concise message outlining my achievements and contributions to the company. Offer tips on how to make a compelling case for a raise while remaining professional and respectful.
  20. Conduct research and provide a comprehensive list of the most effective sales strategies, including cross-selling, upselling, bundling, and referral programs. Offer advice on how to implement these strategies in my business and measure their effectiveness.
  21. Help me identify the strengths and weaknesses of my team members, and develop a tailored plan to improve their skills and knowledge. Provide guidance on how to set SMART goals, create training programs, and track progress.
  22. Write a cover letter for a graphic designer position, emphasizing your creative skills and portfolio.
  23. Generate a personalized objective statement for a human resources resume.
  24. Come up with a list of 5 relevant achievements to include in a marketing manager cover letter.
  25. Generate a tailored 2-minute pitch for an IT project manager job interview.
  26. Write a persuasive email to a potential employer explaining your experience as a sales representative.
  27. Come up with a list of 10 unique qualities to include in an event planner resume.
  28. Generate a 1-page summary of your experiences and accomplishments as a digital marketer.
  29. Write a cover letter addressing the specific qualifications listed for a software development manager position.
  30. Come up with a list of 5 ways to tailor your resume for an accounting job.
  31. Write a compelling property listing for a beachfront villa in Malibu, California.
  32. Generate a list of 10 prospective tenant follow-up messages for a property management company.
  33. Write a persuasive email to a potential buyer for a luxury penthouse in New York City.
  34. Create a 2-minute virtual tour script for a modern condo in downtown Toronto.
  35. Create a list of 5 local schools to mention in a family-friendly neighbourhood guide.
  36. Write a 1-page property brochure for a historic colonial-style home in Boston.
  37. Write a captivating property description for a rustic mountain cabin in Aspen, Colorado.
  38. Come up with a series of 5 Instagram posts to showcase your luxury listings.
  39. Generate a list of 10 home-selling tips for homeowners.
  40. Write a persuasive letter to a property owner about renovating their property before listing with you.
  41. Come up with a list of 5 home remodelling tips for sellers.
  42. Generate a list of 10 potential clients from your social media networks.
  43. Write a persuasive message to a client who is downsizing to a smaller home.
  44. Come up with a series of 5 creative home staging ideas for a small space.
  45. Explain the importance of event planning software and tools, including project management, guest management, and registration.
  46. Write a guide on event decor, including trends, budgeting, and vendor selection.
  47. Explain the different types of event production companies, including audiovisual, lighting, and staging services.
  48. Provide a list of event planning associations and organizations, including benefits and membership requirements.
  49. Offer an in-depth analysis of current event marketing trends, including social media, influencer marketing, and experiential marketing.
  50. Write a guide on event insurance, including types of coverage and how to select the right policy for your event.
  51. Explain the importance of event staffing, including hiring, training, and managing a team for successful event execution.
  52. As a software engineer, design a user interface for a new mobile app, including wireframes, prototypes, and usability testing.
  53. As a financial analyst, conduct a financial analysis for a new investment opportunity, including financial statements, cash flow analysis, and risk assessment.

Personal Assistance

  1. What are some cost-effective ways to improve the curb appeal of my home?
  2. Can you recommend some energy-efficient upgrades to my home to save on utility bills?
  3. What are some DIY projects that I can do to update my bathroom without breaking the bank?
  4. Can you suggest some low-maintenance landscaping ideas for my backyard?
  5. How can I create more storage space in my home without adding square footage?
  6. What are some new movies that are coming out in theatres this month?
  7. Can you recommend a good book for me to read during my upcoming vacation?
  8. I want to plan a weekend getaway with my friends. What are some fun activities we can do in the area?
  9. What are some popular video games that are released this year?
  10. List some funny ways to prank your siblings.
  11. Write a humorous message to your best friend on their wedding day.
  12. Come up with a witty one-liner about pandas.
  13. Write a parody of a popular song about pizza.
  14. What happens when a unicorn and a mermaid go on a date?
  15. Write a short story where a stapler is a protagonist.
  16. Imagine a world where cats can speak. What do they say?
  17. What if aliens landed on Earth and were obsessed with cheeseburgers?
  18. Write a funny dialogue between a pen and a pencil.
  19. Can you recommend a good TV show to binge-watch on Netflix?
  20. What are some effective methods to help my toddler sleep through the night?
  21. My child is having difficulty adjusting to a new school. Can you suggest some strategies to help them cope?
  22. What are some age-appropriate chores that I can assign to my children to teach them responsibility?
  23. Can you recommend some healthy snacks for my child’s lunchbox?
  24. How can I encourage my child to read more and develop a love for books?
  25. I’m planning a trip to Japan and want to experience the cherry blossom season. When is the best time to go?
  26. Can you recommend a guided tour of the ancient ruins in Machu Picchu, Peru?
  27. I want to plan a ski trip to the Swiss Alps for a group of friends in December. Can you suggest some ski resorts and accommodations?
  28. Act as a personal trainer. Help me devise a workout plan for building core strength and stability.
  29. Provide a guide on healthy eating habits for individuals with specific dietary restrictions, such as gluten-free or dairy-free.
  30. Create a 1-month workout plan for improving flexibility and mobility.
  31. Write a comprehensive guide on managing anxiety and stress through mindfulness and meditation techniques.
  32. Provide a list of common mental health disorders and their symptoms, as well as resources for seeking professional help.
  33. Explain the benefits of regular physical activity for cardiovascular health and provide a sample cardio workout plan.
  34. Offer a detailed explanation of the benefits and risks of different types of diets, such as keto and paleo.
  35. Write an in-depth analysis of the current state of mental health care and its impact on marginalized communities.
  36. Provide a list of recommended resources for managing chronic pain and improving quality of life.
  37. Explain the importance of good posture and offer exercises for improving posture and preventing back pain.
  38. Generate some vegan recipe ideas
  39. Create a complete recipe for Vegan Pizza for a single serving
  40. Create a weekly shopping list for the above-mentioned recipe for a single person
  41. Generate a list of vacation ideas that I can go for 5 days from my home city of Melbourne
  42. Generate the complete itinerary for Phillip Island
  43. Create a packing list for this itinerary
  44. Create a 2-week itinerary for a vacation to India
  45. Give me the top 10 stocks in us stock market which has high Dividend yield from 2011 to 2021
  46. I have 100% exposure in equities, what other ways I can look into diversifying my investments
  47. What are some cost-effective ways to improve the facade appeal of my home?
  48. Provide some DIY to improve Landscaping in a total budget of A$1000 with some options from Bunnings
  49. Can you recommend some energy-efficient upgrades to my home to save on utility bills?
  50. I want to plan a weekend getaway with my friends. What are some fun activities we can do in the area?

Conclusion

In conclusion, prompts play a vital role in natural language processing by guiding AI language models to generate specific responses relevant to the given task or topic. The use of prompts has revolutionized the field of NLP, enabling machines to understand and respond to human language more accurately and efficiently. It is important to provide clear and specific prompts to ensure the desired outcome is achieved, and to be aware of potential ambiguities or vagueness in the prompt to avoid generating irrelevant or misleading responses. As NLP continues to evolve, the use of prompts will undoubtedly become more prevalent, allowing us to interact with machines in increasingly natural and intuitive ways.

item.title: Set up your system for DevOps with Ubuntu Linux
item.description:
In today’s fast-paced software development world, DevOps has become an essential approach for creating, testing and delivering software products at a rapid pace. To set up your system for DevOps, you need to have the right tools and applications installed on your system. Setting up your system for DevOps is a crucial step in the software development process, as it can save you time and improve your productivity. In this guide, we will explore the essential tools and applications that you need to install required tools on your system to set it up for DevOps, including development tools, automation tools, and other software applications. With these tools in place, you’ll be ready to start building and delivering high-quality software products with the speed and efficiency of DevOps.

This is continuation to my previous post of setting my system for Development

Git

Git is a free and open-source distributed version control system that was created by Linus Torvalds in 2005. It was designed to be fast, efficient, and scalable, making it ideal for managing both small and large software development projects.

Git allows developers to track changes in source code, collaborate with others, and maintain a history of project revisions. It accomplishes this by allowing multiple users to work on the same codebase simultaneously, while also providing a system for managing changes to the codebase and reconciling any conflicts that may arise.

To Install git you can use the apt package manager with the following command.

One of the main advantages of Git is its ability to create and manage branches, which allows developers to experiment with new features and ideas without affecting the main codebase. Git also provides a robust set of tools for merging and integrating these changes back into the main codebase, enabling teams to work together effectively on complex software projects.

This is the first step in setting up your system for DevOps.

oh-my-zsh

oh-my-zsh is an open-source framework for managing the Zsh shell. It was created by Robby Russell in 2009 and has since become one of the most popular Zsh frameworks available. Oh My Zsh is designed to make it easy to configure and customize your Zsh shell, with a range of plugins, themes, and settings that can be easily added and managed.

One of the main advantages of Oh My Zsh is its extensive library of plugins, which can be used to add additional functionality and features to your Zsh shell. These plugins cover a wide range of use cases, from auto-completion and syntax highlighting to Git integration and custom aliases.

In addition to its plugins, Oh My Zsh also includes a range of customizable themes that can be used to change the look and feel of your Zsh shell. Themes can be easily installed and customized, allowing you to create a personalized and professional-looking shell environment.

Oh My Zsh is also highly customizable, with a range of settings and options that can be adjusted to suit your specific needs and preferences. Whether you’re a novice user or an experienced developer, Oh My Zsh offers a powerful and flexible framework for managing your Zsh shell and enhancing your productivity.

To Install oh-my-zsh you can follow these steps, you need to have curl and zsh before you can install oh-my-zsh

Docker

Docker is an open-source containerization platform that is designed to make it easier to create, deploy, and run applications in a variety of environments. It was created in 2013 and has since become one of the most popular tools for containerization.

Docker enables developers to package their applications and dependencies into self-contained, portable containers, which can be run on any system that supports Docker. These containers are lightweight, fast, and secure, making them ideal for use in cloud environments, as well as in local development and testing.

One of the key advantages of Docker is its ability to simplify the deployment and management of applications. With Docker, developers can create a containerized application once, and then deploy it across multiple environments, without having to worry about compatibility issues or configuration differences.

Installation of Docker in Linux use the following command in Ubuntu Linux

VirtualBox

VirtualBox is a free and open-source virtualization software that allows users to run multiple operating systems on a single computer. It was created by Oracle Corporation in 2007 and has since become one of the most popular virtualization tools available.

VirtualBox works by creating a virtual environment in which you can install and run multiple operating systems, including Windows, Linux, and macOS. This virtual environment is isolated from your host operating system, which means that any changes or modifications you make to your virtual environment will not affect your host operating system.

One of the main advantages of VirtualBox is its versatility. It can be used for a wide range of applications, from testing software and developing applications to running legacy software and experimenting with new operating systems. With VirtualBox, you can create and manage virtual machines with ease, allowing you to run multiple operating systems simultaneously and switch between them with just a few clicks.

To install VirtualBox, I am going to follow these steps

  • Add the following line to the /etc/apt/sources.list
  • Download the public key and install it
  • Install VirtualBox

Vagrant

Vagrant is a popular open-source tool that is used for creating and managing virtual development environments. It was first released in 2010 by Mitchell Hashimoto and has since become a key tool for software developers and IT professionals alike.

Vagrant works by automating the creation and configuration of virtual machines, allowing developers to easily create and manage their development environments with ease. With Vagrant, developers can define their development environment in a simple configuration file, which can be easily shared and replicated across multiple systems.

One of the main advantages of Vagrant is its flexibility. It supports a wide range of virtualization providers, including VirtualBox, VMware, and Hyper-V, allowing developers to choose the best virtualization platform for their specific needs. Vagrant also supports a wide range of operating systems, including Linux, Windows, and macOS, making it a versatile tool for developers working in different environments.

Use the following links to install Vagrant on your Linux box.

Wireshark

Wireshark is a free and open-source network protocol analyzer that allows users to capture, analyze, and troubleshoot network traffic. It was first released in 1998 as Ethereal, but was later renamed to Wireshark in 2006.

Wireshark works by capturing packets of network data and displaying them in a user-friendly interface. It supports a wide range of protocols, including Ethernet, Wi-Fi, TCP/IP, and HTTP, making it a versatile tool for network administrators and security professionals.

One of the main advantages of Wireshark is its ability to help troubleshoot network problems. With Wireshark, network administrators can quickly identify and isolate network issues, such as misconfigured routers, network congestion, or security breaches. Wireshark also provides a range of advanced features, such as the ability to filter and analyze network traffic, making it a powerful tool for network analysis and optimization.

Installation is simple, you just need to install Wireshark, tshark and Tcpdump

This step is essential in setting up your system for DevOps as networking is a key factor of DevOps and as a DevOps engineer, you need to be aware of how the network is performing.

Python

Python is a popular high-level programming language that is widely used in a variety of industries and applications. Created in the late 1980s by Guido van Rossum, Python has since become one of the most popular programming languages in the world, with a large and active community of developers and users.

One of the main advantages of Python is its simplicity and ease of use. Its clear and concise syntax makes it easy to read and write, making it an ideal choice for beginners and experienced developers alike. Python also has a vast standard library, which includes a wide range of modules and tools that can be used to develop a wide range of applications, from web development to scientific computing.

Another advantage of Python is its versatility. It can be used for a wide range of applications, from building desktop applications and web applications to data analysis and machine learning. Python also has a range of third-party libraries and frameworks, such as Django, Flask, NumPy, and TensorFlow, which can be used to extend its functionality and support a wide range of use cases.

Python3 is already installed with the latest releases of Ubuntu

Python is also highly portable, with versions available for all major operating systems, including Windows, Linux, and macOS. Its ease of use, versatility, and portability have made it a popular choice for developers and organizations of all sizes, making Python an essential tool for many different industries and applications.

Python plays a key factor in the automation of the system and thus it is must-have in setting up your system for DevOps.

Ansible

Ansible is an open-source IT automation tool that is designed to simplify the deployment and management of software and infrastructure. It was created by Michael DeHaan in 2012 and has since become one of the most popular automation tools available.

Ansible works by using simple YAML-based configuration files to define tasks and workflows, making it easy for developers and IT professionals to automate complex processes. It can be used to automate a wide range of tasks, including software deployment, configuration management, and infrastructure provisioning.

One of the main advantages of Ansible is its simplicity. It does not require any special agents or software to be installed on remote systems and can be easily configured and managed from a central control node. This makes it a highly scalable and flexible tool for managing large and complex environments.

To Install Ansible, follow these steps

Terraform

Terraform is an open-source infrastructure-as-code tool that is designed to automate the provisioning, deployment, and management of cloud-based infrastructure. Created by HashiCorp in 2014, Terraform has become a popular tool for DevOps engineers, cloud architects, and IT professionals.

Terraform works by defining infrastructure as code, which allows developers to easily manage complex cloud environments using simple configuration files. With Terraform, users can define and manage infrastructure resources, such as virtual machines, networks, and storage, across a wide range of cloud providers, including AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud Platform.

One of the main advantages of Terraform is its ability to provide a single, unified interface for managing cloud infrastructure. This enables users to manage complex cloud environments more efficiently and effectively, without the need for multiple tools or platforms.

Terraform also provides a range of advanced features, such as support for version control, testing, and automation, making it a powerful tool for managing cloud infrastructure at scale. Whether you’re a DevOps engineer, cloud architect, or IT professional, Terraform is a versatile and reliable tool that can help you automate and streamline your cloud infrastructure management.

To install Terraform use the following commands

AWS CLI

The AWS Command Line Interface (CLI) is an open-source tool that allows developers and IT professionals to interact with Amazon Web Services (AWS) from the command line. Created by Amazon in 2013, the AWS CLI has become a popular tool for managing and automating AWS services.

The AWS CLI works by providing a simple, unified interface for interacting with AWS services, making it easy to manage and automate AWS resources from the command line. With the AWS CLI, users can perform a wide range of tasks, such as creating and managing EC2 instances, managing S3 buckets, and configuring IAM policies.

One of the main advantages of the AWS CLI is its flexibility. It can be used on a wide range of platforms, including Windows, macOS, and Linux, and can be easily integrated with other command line tools and scripts. This makes it a highly versatile tool for managing and automating AWS services.

VS Code & VIM

VS Code

Visual Studio Code is a lightweight but powerful source code editor which runs on your desktop and is available for Windows, macOS and Linux. It comes with built-in support for JavaScript, TypeScript and Node.js and has a rich ecosystem of extensions for other languages and runtimes (such as C++, C#, Java, Python, PHP, Go, .NET)

To install VS code download the package and install it. You can use the dpkg command to install the package manager.

Vim

Vim is a highly configurable, open-source text editor that is known for its speed, efficiency, and versatility. Originally released in 1991, Vim is a descendant of the popular Vi editor and is designed to work in a terminal environment, making it ideal for use on servers or other systems without graphical interfaces.

One of the things that sets Vim apart from other text editors is its extensive range of keyboard shortcuts and commands. These shortcuts allow experienced Vim users to navigate, edit, and manipulate text with incredible speed and precision, often without ever having to take their hands off the keyboard.

Another key feature of Vim is its high level of configuration. Users can customize the editor to suit their specific needs and preferences, from changing the appearance of the interface to defining their own key mappings and commands.

Despite its initial learning curve, Vim has developed a passionate following among developers, sysadmins, and other power users who value its speed, efficiency, and customizability. Whether you’re working on a remote server or just looking for a powerful text editor that can keep up with your workflow, Vim is definitely worth considering.

Conclusion

In conclusion, setting up your system for DevOps can greatly improve your productivity and efficiency as a developer or IT professional. By installing the right tools and software, you can streamline your development workflows, automate repetitive tasks, and manage your infrastructure more effectively.

From version control systems like Git to automation tools like Ansible, there are many open-source tools available that can help you simplify and optimize your DevOps workflows. By choosing the right tools for your needs and customizing your Ubuntu system accordingly, you can create a powerful and efficient development environment that meets your specific requirements.

So, whether you’re just getting started with DevOps or looking to improve your existing workflows, be sure to take the time to set up your Ubuntu system with the right tools and software. With the right setup, you can streamline your development processes, reduce your workload, and achieve greater success in your projects.

item.title: Setting up my new PC with Ubuntu as my primary development workstation
item.description:
Namaste! In this article, I will be sharing my experience of setting up my PC with Linux for development purposes. As a developer, I have always been fascinated by the open-source community and the flexibility it offers.

While Linux has been my primary operating system of choice, I have also used Windows and Mac OS from time to time for specific purposes. However, my love for Linux has always drawn me back to this open-source operating system. The flexibility, security, and control that Linux offers have always appealed to me as a developer. In fact, I have found that Linux has been instrumental in improving my productivity and efficiency as a programmer. With its vast array of tools and resources, Linux has enabled me to take on complex coding projects with ease. In this article, I will be discussing my recent decision to switch back to Linux as my main operating system for development purposes, and the steps I took to set up my PC for Linux.

In this article, I will be discussing the reasons behind my decision, the steps I took to set up Linux on my PC, and the benefits that I have experienced since the transition. Whether you are a seasoned developer or just starting out, I hope that my experience will inspire you to explore the world of Linux and its many advantages. So, let’s dive in and get started!

(PS! — I am continuously updating this list)

Browsers

Firefox

Firefox has been my preferred browser for many years now. The open-source philosophy of Firefox, coupled with its robust security features and customizable interface, have always appealed to me. As a developer, I rely heavily on various web development tools, and Firefox offers a plethora of add-ons that cater to my needs. Whether it’s the Web Developer add-on for debugging and analysing websites, or the Firebug add-on for inspecting HTML, CSS, and JavaScript in real-time, Firefox has always been my go-to browser for web development.

One of the things I appreciate most about Firefox is its commitment to user privacy. The browser comes with built-in privacy features that help protect my online identity and sensitive information from third-party trackers and hackers. Moreover, Firefox is constantly updated with the latest security patches, ensuring that I can browse the web without any worries.

Another feature that I find particularly useful is Firefox’s ability to sync my bookmarks, passwords, and browsing history across different devices. As someone who works on multiple computers and devices, this feature has been a lifesaver, allowing me to seamlessly access my bookmarks and saved passwords from anywhere.

Overall, Firefox has been an integral part of my web development workflow, and I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a secure, customizable, and privacy-focused browser.

The best part it is included in the Ubuntu package.

Chromium

While Firefox is a powerful and versatile browser, there are some limitations that may has forced me to use Google Chrome instead. One such limitation is the lack of support for some websites and web applications that are optimized specifically for Google Chrome or Chromium. This is because some web developers may focus their efforts on optimizing their sites for Chrome, which can result in compatibility issues with other browsers like Firefox.

Another limitation of Firefox is that it may consume more system resources than Google Chrome, particularly when running multiple tabs or extensions. This can result in slower performance and longer load times, especially on older or less powerful computers.

Source code management (SCM)

Git

Git is a distributed version control system that is both free and open-source, and it is capable of handling projects of varying sizes — from small to very large — with exceptional speed and efficiency. Git is user-friendly and lightweight, with outstanding performance 

I mostly use Git with GitHub as my primary source code management system.Git is already installed with most of the Ubuntu systems but you can use the apt package manager if you want to install git.

Notes taking

Obsidian

Obsidian is a powerful note-taking and knowledge-management tool. As someone who regularly takes notes and conducts research, I have always been on the lookout for a tool that can help me organize my thoughts and ideas more effectively. That’s where Obsidian comes in — it’s a feature-rich, cross-platform note-taking application that allows you to create and organize notes in a way that makes sense to you.

The biggest advantage of using obsidian is that it is abile to create a network of interconnected notes, making it easy to navigate and discover new connections between ideas. Using the application’s graph view, you can visualize the connections between your notes, helping you to identify patterns and relationships that might not be immediately apparent.

Obsidian can be installed as a snap package in Ubuntu.

Joplin

Joplin is a popular open-source note-taking and to-do application gaining traction in recent years. With its intuitive user interface, cross-platform compatibility, and powerful features, Joplin has become a favourite among users looking for a reliable and customizable note-taking tool.

One of the unique features of Joplin is its support for end-to-end encryption, which allows you to protect your notes and other sensitive information from prying eyes. This feature is particularly valuable for users who need to store confidential or sensitive data, such as passwords, financial information, or personal details.

Another advantage of Joplin is its support for Markdown formatting, which enables users to create richly formatted notes that are easy to read and organize. With support for tags, notebooks, and the ability to create custom templates, Joplin makes it easy to organize and manage your notes in a way that makes sense to you.

Joplin also offers a range of synchronization options, allowing you to store your notes on your local machine, on a remote server, or in the cloud. This flexibility makes it easy to access your notes from anywhere, on any device.

On Linux, the recommended way is to use the following installation script as it will handle the desktop icon too:

Video recording & Editing

OBS

OBS — Open Broadcaster Software, a popular and powerful live streaming and video recording software used by content creators around the world. Whether you’re a gamer, musician, or just someone looking to share your thoughts and ideas with the world, OBS offers a range of features and tools to help you create and share high-quality content.

One of the things that sets OBS apart from other live streaming and video recording software is its open-source nature. This means that the software is free to use and can be customized and modified by the community to suit their specific needs. It also means that the software is constantly evolving and improving, with new features and updates being added all the time.

In addition to its open-source nature, OBS is also incredibly versatile and customizable. Whether you’re streaming to Twitch, YouTube, or another platform, OBS offers a range of settings and options to help you optimize your stream and ensure the best possible quality. From adjusting your bitrate and encoding settings to setting up custom scenes and transitions, OBS gives you full control over your stream.

SimpleScreenRecorder

SimpleScreenRecorder is a Linux program that was created by Maarten Baert to record programs and games.

Maarten Baert’s original goal was to create a program that was just really simple to use, but he was writing it he started adding more and more features. The result is actually a pretty powerful program. It’s ‘simple’ in the sense that it’s easier to use than ffmpeg/avconv or VLC, because it has a straightforward user interface.

Shotcut

Charlie Yates, the co-founder of MLT and the original lead developer, first came up with the idea for Shotcut in November 2004 (see the original website). Since then, the software has undergone a complete rewrite by another MLT co-founder and its current lead developer, Dan Dennedy. 

Dan’s vision for Shotcut was to create a new video editor based on MLT that could take advantage of the latest cross-platform capabilities, as well as the powerful WebVfx and Movit plugins. While Dan chose to reuse the name Shotcut due to his fondness for it, the current version of the software is a complete rewrite of the original.

snap install shotcut --classic

KdenLive

Kdenlive is a video editor that operates on open-source principles. Its development was initiated around 2003, and it is constructed on Qt and the KDE Frameworks libraries. The majority of video processing is executed through the MLT Framework, which relies on a range of other open-source initiatives such as FFmpeg, frei0r, movit, ladspa, sox, among others.

Kdenlive is designed to cater to a diverse range of video editing requirements, from fundamental to professional-level tasks.

To download and install Kdenlive, use the apt package manager

IDE & Text editors

VS Code

Visual Studio Code is a lightweight but powerful source code editor which runs on your desktop and is available for Windows, macOS and Linux. It comes with built-in support for JavaScript, TypeScript and Node.js and has a rich ecosystem of extensions for other languages and runtimes (such as C++, C#, Java, Python, PHP, Go, .NET)

To install VS code download the package and install it. You can use the dpkg command to install the package manager.

Vim

Vim is a highly configurable, open-source text editor that is known for its speed, efficiency, and versatility. Originally released in 1991, Vim is a descendant of the popular Vi editor and is designed to work in a terminal environment, making it ideal for use on servers or other systems without graphical interfaces.

One of the things that sets Vim apart from other text editors is its extensive range of keyboard shortcuts and commands. These shortcuts allow experienced Vim users to navigate, edit, and manipulate text with incredible speed and precision, often without ever having to take their hands off the keyboard.

Another key feature of Vim is its high level of configuration. Users can customize the editor to suit their specific needs and preferences, from changing the appearance of the interface to defining their own key mappings and commands.

Despite its initial learning curve, Vim has developed a passionate following among developers, sysadmins, and other power users who value its speed, efficiency, and customizability. Whether you’re working on a remote server or just looking for a powerful text editor that can keep up with your workflow, Vim is definitely worth considering.

item.title: Setup WordPress using AWS Lightsail
item.description:
Amazon Lightsail is a user-friendly cloud computing platform that allows you to easily manage your web applications, websites, and other services. If you are looking to set up a new WordPress blog or website using AWS Lightsail you can follow the below-mentioned steps.

Setting up your VPS

If you are looking to set up a new WordPress blog or website using AWS Lightsail you can follow the below-mentioned steps.

When you log in to your AWS account, you can search for Lightsail

Lightsail in the AWS console

  1. Log in to your AWS Lightsail account and click on the “Create instance” button.
  2. Select the “WordPress” blueprint from the list of options.

  1. Choose the instance location, instance plan, and instance name. You can also choose to add a launch script if you have one.
  2. Click on the “Create instance” button to start the instance.
  3. Once the instance is running, click on the “Connect using SSH” button to connect to the instance.
  4. Once you’re connected to the instance, run the following command to configure WordPress
    • cat bitnami_credentials
  5. This will provide you the credentials.

DNS

One of the key features of Lightsail is the ability to manage your own domain name system (DNS) settings. DNS is responsible for translating human-readable domain names (such as example.com) into IP addresses, allowing users to access websites and services using easy-to-remember domain names instead of complex numerical IP addresses. In this guide, we’ll explore how to manage your DNS settings in Amazon Lightsail, including how to create and edit DNS records, and how to configure your domain to work with your Lightsail resources.

To configure DNS, follow the following steps

  • Select Create a DNS Zone

  • Put your Domain name
  • Configure the name servers in the DNS
  • Configure the IP assignment (Note you would need either IPv4 or IPv6 for this)
  • Make sure you add both entries for www as well.

Setting up a certificate for HTTPS {.wp-block-heading}

In this guide, we’ll explore the process of securing traffic to and from your Bitnami application host using SSL certificates from Let’s Encrypt.

Let’s Encrypt is a Certificate Authority (CA) that provides free SSL certificates and I will provide a step-by-step tutorial on how to generate a Let’s Encrypt SSL certificate for your domain.

Run the following command

sudo /opt/bitnami/bncert-tool

It will ask you the domain name

Follow the steps as shown in this video and you should be able to set up your HTTPS certificate.

Set up email with Lightsail

This is the most complex part of the setup.

  • Click on the “SMTP Settings” tab in the left-hand menu.
  • Click on the “Create My SMTP Credentials” button.

  • Enter a name for your SMTP credentials in the “Create a new IAM user” field.
  • Click on the “Create” button.

  • Your SMTP credentials will be displayed on the screen. Make note of the SMTP username and password as you will need them to configure your email client.
  • Click on the “Download Credentials” button to save the SMTP credentials in a secure location.

  • Click on the “Close” button to exit the SMTP Settings page.

For this firstly you need to configure the identities in the Amazon SES

  • Go to the verified identities
  • Enter the domain that you want to verify, and choose to Create an identity.
  • Once you’ve set up your domain identity using Easy DKIM, you’ll need to complete the verification process with DKIM authentication. This involves copying the CNAME records generated by Amazon SES and publishing them to your domain’s DNS provider. Please note that it may take up to 72 hours for the records to be detected.
  • To add the DNS records to your domain’s DNS zone, open a new browser tab and navigate to the Lightsail console.
  • From the Lightsail home page, go to the Networking tab and select your domain’s DNS zone.
  • Follow the instructions provided by Amazon SES to add the DNS records to your domain’s DNS zone.
  • Configure the SMTP configuration in WordPress using the wp-SMTP plugin

Now login to your WordPress Instance and configure it.

Congratulations! You have successfully set up a WordPress blog with Lightsail in AWS. With your new blog up and running, you can start sharing your thoughts, ideas, and expertise with the world.

Remember to keep your blog secure by regularly updating your WordPress installation and plugins, and by following best practices for online security. You can also use Lightsail’s built-in security features, such as firewall rules and SSH key pairs, to further enhance your blog’s security.

Finally, don’t forget to regularly back up your blog to ensure that your data is safe in case of any unexpected issues. Lightsail makes it easy to set up automatic backups and restore your blog if needed.

Thanks for following along with this tutorial, and I hope you enjoy using your new WordPress blog!

item.title: Vagrant — The complete tutorial
item.description:
If you are looking for a complete course you can subscribe to this course.

<div class="ufwp"><div class="ufwp-standard"><div class="ufwp-course ufwp-course--sale" data-ufwp-course-id="5080200"><a class="ufwp-course__link" href="https://www.udemy.com/course/vagrant-the-ultimate-course/" target="_blank" rel="nofollow" title="Vagrant - A hands-on course"><span class="ufwp-course__img"><img src="https://img-c.udemycdn.com/course/480x270/5080200_7247.jpg" alt="Vagrant - A hands-on course"></span><span class="ufwp-course__content"><span class="ufwp-course__title">Vagrant - A hands-on course</span><span class="ufwp-course__instructors">Mohit Kumar, 12 years experience in Solution Design and Architecture</span><span class="ufwp-course__headline">Use Vagrant to spin Virtual Machines, write Vagrantfiles and understand usage of Vagrant boxes and plugins</span><span class="ufwp-course__footer"><span class="ufwp-course__price ufwp-course__price--list">A$26.99</span><span class="ufwp-course__price">A$19.99</span><span class="ufwp-course__rating"><span class="ufwp-star-rating"><span style="width: 100%;"></span></span> 5 (3 ratings)</span></span></span></a></div></div></div>

Installation

Windows

  1. Visit the Vagrant downloads page at https://www.vagrantup.com/downloads.html
  2. Scroll down to the Windows section and select the appropriate installer for your system, either 32-bit or 64-bit.
  3. Once the download is complete, double-click the installer to begin the installation process.
  4. Follow the prompts in the installation wizard to complete the installation. The default installation location is usually fine, but you can choose a custom location if you prefer.
  5. After the installation is complete, open the Command Prompt by pressing the Windows key + R, typing “cmd” and pressing Enter.
  6. In the Command Prompt, type “vagrant” and press Enter. This will confirm that Vagrant has been installed correctly.

Linux – Debian

  1. Open a terminal window by pressing Ctrl + Alt + T.
  2. Update your system’s package list by running the following commands:

Vagrantfile

A Vagrantfile is a configuration file used by Vagrant, a tool for managing and creating virtual machine environments. The Vagrantfile contains all the instructions necessary to create and configure a virtual machine (VM) according to specific requirements.

The Vagrantfile is essentially a Ruby script that defines the settings for a VM, including its base box (which specifies the operating system and software), the amount of RAM and CPU allocated to the VM, network settings, shared folders, and any additional software to be installed.

By specifying all these settings in the Vagrantfile, Vagrant can easily create and configure a new VM with a single command, allowing developers to quickly set up and tear down development environments. The Vagrantfile can also be version-controlled and shared with other developers, ensuring consistency and reproducibility of development environments.
